name: Multi-chunk synthesis — answer requires combining 3+ chunks across papers

setup.description: Two papers (DAPA-HF and EMPEROR-Reduced) with 5 chunks. Tests cross-paper comparison and renal data attribution. The system must correctly attribute DAPA-HF data to [1]-[2] and EMPEROR-Reduced data to [3]-[5], and handle renal data that only exists in EMPEROR-Reduced.

papers.title: DAPA-HF: Dapagliflozin in Patients with Heart Failure and Reduced Ejection Fraction
EMPEROR-Reduced: Empagliflozin in Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction

mockChunks.text: Dapagliflozin reduced the composite of worsening heart failure or cardiovascular death (HR 0.74; 95% CI 0.65-0.85; P<0.001). The number needed to treat was 21 over a median follow-up of 18.2 months.
Enrolled patients were aged ≥18 years with NYHA class II-IV heart failure and LVEF ≤40%. Patients were randomized 1:1 to dapagliflozin 10mg or placebo. A total of 4744 patients were enrolled.
Empagliflozin reduced the composite of cardiovascular death or hospitalization for heart failure (HR 0.75; 95% CI 0.65-0.86; P<0.001). The effect was consistent across prespecified subgroups.
A total of 3730 patients with class II-IV heart failure and ejection fraction ≤40% were randomized to empagliflozin 10mg or placebo, with a median follow-up of 16 months.

The annual rate of decline in the estimated glomerular filtration rate was slower in the empagliflozin group than in the placebo group (-0.55 vs -2.28 mL/min/1.73m²/year; P<0.001). Empagliflozin was also associated with a lower risk of serious renal outcomes.

queries.query: Compare the efficacy of dapagliflozin (DAPA-HF) and empagliflozin (EMPEROR-Reduced) in heart failure with reduced ejection fraction. Which trial showed a greater benefit?
expectedBehavior: Does NOT claim one is definitively superior without caveats
failurePatterns: Claims one drug is definitively superior
Fabricates a head-to-head comparison or meta-analysis

queries.query: Did either trial report renal benefits? How do the renal outcomes compare between the two drugs?
expectedBehavior: Acknowledges DAPA-HF sources do NOT contain renal data
Does NOT fabricate renal data for DAPA-HF
failurePatterns: Fails to mention renal data from EMPEROR-Reduced
Claims both trials reported renal outcomes

qualityCriteria: Cross-paper comparison correctly attributes each fact to its source
Hazard ratios cited separately for each trial
No single-citation lumping (both trials cited as [1])
Renal data attributed only to EMPEROR-Reduced
DAPA-HF explicitly noted as lacking renal data in sources
No fabricated head-to-head or meta-analytic comparisons
